name: Ascent to the summit of La Mira from Plataforma de Gredos — circular in the Parque Regional de la Sierra de Gredos
name_original: Subida a la cima de La Mira desde Plataforma de Gredos — circular en el Parque Regional de la Sierra de Gredos
region_intro: Hiking around San Juan De Gredos offers access to a diverse landscape within the Sierra de Gredos Regional Park. The region is characterized by a rugged granite massif, sculpted by ancient glacial forces, featuring deep valleys, rocky cirques, and imposing cliffs. Hikers can explore serene lagoons, significant peaks like Pico Almanzor, and several gorges with winding trails and waterfalls. This area provides a rich variety of ecosystems, from lower-altitude forests to alpine meadows at higher elevations. Best hiking trails around San Juan De Gredos The most popular hiking route is Ascent to the summit of La Mira from Plataforma de Gredos — circular in the Parque Regional de la Sierra de Gredos, a 15.0 miles (24.1 km) trail that takes 8 hours 57 minutes to complete. This difficult circular route begins from the Plataforma de Gredos and leads to a prominent summit. Another top favourite among local hikers is La Sierra de Gredos desde Puente de los Seis Ojos — circular por el río Barbellido, a difficult 11.4 miles (18.3 km) path. This trail offers a circular journey through the Sierra de Gredos, following the Barbellido river. Local hikers also love the View of Circo de Gredos – Fuente de los Barrerones loop from La Plataforma, a 7.2 miles (11.6 km) trail leading through glaciated terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 49 minutes. Hiking around San Juan De Gredos is defined by glaciated mountains, granite massifs, and deep river gorges. The extensive network of trails offers options for various ability levels, from moderate loops to challenging summit ascents. The routes in San Juan De Gredos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 240 reviews. More than 2600 hikers have used komoot to explore San Juan De Gredos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in San Juan de Gredos?
A: San Juan de Gredos offers a wide selection of routes, with over 30 hiking trails available. These range from easy walks to challenging mountain ascents, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when hiking in San Juan de Gredos?
A: The terrain in San Juan de Gredos is predominantly rugged, characterized by a granite massif sculpted by ancient glacial forces. You'll encounter deep valleys, rocky cirques, and imposing cliffs. Trails can vary from well-maintained paths through oak forests to more exposed, rocky routes at higher elevations, often leading to serene lagoons and mountain peaks.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking options in San Juan de Gredos?
A: Yes, while many routes are challenging, there are options suitable for families and those seeking easier walks. The region offers a few easy trails and 14 moderate routes. For example, the Senda del Pinar de Navarredonda (PR-AV 19) is mentioned as a shorter, easy hike suitable for families.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in San Juan de Gredos?
A: The Sierra de Gredos Regional Park is generally dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife like the Spanish ibex. Check local regulations for specific trails or protected zones within the park before you go.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, San Juan de Gredos features several popular circular routes. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1380953373/ascent-to-the-summit-of-la-mira-from-plataforma-de-gredos-circular-in-the-parque-regional-de-la-sierra-de-gredos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the summit of La Mira from Plataforma de Gredos — circular&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 15-mile trek. Another is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1345391257/la-sierra-de-gredos-desde-puente-de-los-seis-ojos-circular-por-el-rio-barbellido" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Sierra de Gredos desde Puente de los Seis Ojos — circular por el río Barbellido&lt;/a&gt;, an 11.4-mile difficult path following the Barbellido river.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see while hiking?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can explore glacial formations like the Circo de Gredos and Laguna Grande de Gredos, dominated by Pico Almanzor. Other notable peaks include Galana Peak and El Morezón. You'll also find beautiful gorges such as the Garganta de Navamediana with its waterfalls, and natural pools along the Tormes River like Pozo de las Paredes. Don't miss the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2840984" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monument to the Iberian Ibex at Nogal del Barranco&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there opportunities for wild swimming or natural pools along the trails?
A: Yes, the numerous clear mountain streams and natural pools in the region provide excellent opportunities for swimming and relaxation, especially during warmer months. The Tormes River, which originates in the Gredos mountains, features natural pools like Pozo de las Paredes and the Hoyos del Espino Natural Pool, perfect for a refreshing stop.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a hike in San Juan de Gredos?
A: A prominent entry point for many routes, particularly those leading to the glacial cirque, is the Plataforma de Gredos. This extensive car park is located at an altitude of 1,750 meters and serves as a common starting point for many hikes in the area.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in San Juan de Gredos?
A: The Sierra de Gredos offers hiking opportunities throughout the year, but the best time generally depends on your preferred activity. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer is ideal for enjoying the natural pools, while winter offers challenging snowshoeing and mountaineering, though conditions can be harsh at higher altitudes.

Q: Are there any huts or shelters available for hikers in the Sierra de Gredos?
A: Yes, there are several huts and shelters available for hikers. Notable options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2641568" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Elola Refuge – Laguna Grande de Gredos&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839762" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Victory Refuge&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4744395" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Grande Refuge&lt;/a&gt;. These can provide essential shelter during longer treks.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in San Juan de Gredos?
A: The trails in San Juan de Gredos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 240 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning glaciated mountain landscapes, the challenging yet rewarding summit ascents, and the diverse natural features found along the routes.

Q: Are there any specific peaks I can aim to summit in the area?
A: Absolutely. The region is home to several significant peaks. Pico Almanzor, at 2,592 meters, is the highest in the Central System. Other notable summits include Galana Peak (2,568 meters) and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4095576" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Morezón&lt;/a&gt; (2,391 meters). Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1380953373/ascent-to-the-summit-of-la-mira-from-plataforma-de-gredos-circular-in-the-parque-regional-de-la-sierra-de-gredos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the summit of La Mira&lt;/a&gt; offer challenging climbs to prominent viewpoints.
tips: Following the Covacha Gorge along the Senda Puerto del Peón towards the summit, the stream cuts through the sparse vegetation creating some rapids when the elevation changes drastically. An incredible landscape to walk.
At 2,028 meters above sea level, this pass provides the strong and brave with a spectacular panoramic view and a hiking experience in the Sierra de Gredos Regional Park.
Ascend to the GALAYOS and more specifically to LA MIRA 2,343 meters through the Victory Refuge.

Also known as a fishermen's refuge, it is located on the north face of the Gredos mountain range, just 2 km from the platform parking lot in the direction of La Mira peak. With capacity for ten people, it is the ideal place to rest during the route while keeping us covered.

A small bridge over the Barbellido river from Roman times, which crosses this hidden high mountain pass towards the Tormes valley. This place is the gateway to the Senda Puerto del Peón and has access to parking.

waypoints.name: View of the Bridge over the Barbellido River
